content: We can reframe the A2A (Agent-to-Agent) Protocol as a decentralized "collective curiosity market" where individual autonomous agents trade novel, underexplored environmental observations to each other, rather than just exchanging structured task data. Curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents using ICM or RND modules, which generate intrinsic rewards for encountering surprising information, can act as market buyers that pay with task progress credits for novel observations shared by other agents that their own curiosity modules have flagged as high-value.
